<findings>

[observation] [info] High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
[observation] [info] Five files with perfect cohesion and zero coupling represent a disciplined, minimally scoped integration layer — each file owns exactly one architectural seam, avoiding the sprawl common in integration suites.
[observation] [info] hench-rex-gateway-pipeline.test.js provides the only integration-level validation of the rex-gateway used by hench — if this file is ever skipped or removed, the most complex cross-package dependency in the system loses its contract test.
[observation] [info] web-server-viewer-boundary.test.js complements the 'Web Boundary Tests' zone (algorithmicId: web-integration) — confirm these are not duplicating coverage of the same boundary; if they are, consolidation would reduce maintenance burden.
[suggestion] [info] Rename gateway-pipeline.test.js to a scoped name that identifies its consumer and upstream package (e.g. web-rex-gateway-pipeline.test.js or web-domain-gateway-pipeline.test.js) to match the naming convention established by hench-rex-gateway-pipeline.test.js and make the suite's gateway coverage map self-documenting
[suggestion] [warning] The REQUIRED_CONTRACTS weak-match means the llm-gateway contract (packages/hench/src/prd/llm-gateway.ts, 30 functions + 10 types) is not independently verified at integration level; add a dedicated assertion block in cross-package-contracts.test.js that imports and exercises at least one symbol from the llm-gateway to close the gap the policy test currently fails to detect

</findings>

<insights>

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
- scheduler-startup.test.js is marked as a required test in CLAUDE.md (sole scheduler boot coverage) — its removal would leave the web task scheduler completely untested at the integration level
- The five files map precisely to the five highest-risk seams in the architecture: cross-package contracts, the rex gateway pipeline, the hench-rex gateway pipeline, the scheduler, and the web server/viewer boundary — good threat-model alignment
- Zero coupling to production zones confirms these tests operate through public APIs only, which means they will catch regressions caused by internal refactoring without being brittle to implementation details
- Five files with perfect cohesion and zero coupling represent a disciplined, minimally scoped integration layer — each file owns exactly one architectural seam, avoiding the sprawl common in integration suites.
- web-server-viewer-boundary.test.js complements the 'Web Boundary Tests' zone (algorithmicId: web-integration) — confirm these are not duplicating coverage of the same boundary; if they are, consolidation would reduce maintenance burden.
- hench-rex-gateway-pipeline.test.js provides the only integration-level validation of the rex-gateway used by hench — if this file is ever skipped or removed, the most complex cross-package dependency in the system loses its contract test.
- The 5-file integration suite is exactly at the MIN_INTEGRATION_FILES floor enforced by integration-coverage-policy.test.js; the proportional-ratio check (15% of e2e count) produces a floor of max(5, ceil(20×0.15))=5, meaning the suite can absorb up to 13 additional E2E test files before the proportional floor rises above 5 — this provides headroom but also means the ratio check offers no incremental pressure to grow the integration suite in the near term
- gateway-pipeline.test.js exists alongside hench-rex-gateway-pipeline.test.js but its scope is not evident from the filename — if gateway-pipeline.test.js covers the web gateway pipelines while hench-rex-gateway-pipeline.test.js covers the hench-rex seam, the naming asymmetry (one scoped, one unscoped) makes the suite's coverage map harder to read than if both files followed the '{consumer}-{upstream}-gateway-pipeline' pattern
- Rename gateway-pipeline.test.js to a scoped name that identifies its consumer and upstream package (e.g. web-rex-gateway-pipeline.test.js or web-domain-gateway-pipeline.test.js) to match the naming convention established by hench-rex-gateway-pipeline.test.js and make the suite's gateway coverage map self-documenting
- The REQUIRED_CONTRACTS weak-match means the llm-gateway contract (packages/hench/src/prd/llm-gateway.ts, 30 functions + 10 types) is not independently verified at integration level; add a dedicated assertion block in cross-package-contracts.test.js that imports and exercises at least one symbol from the llm-gateway to close the gap the policy test currently fails to detect
- [call graph] 30 internal calls, 0 outgoing, 0 incoming (cohesion: 1, coupling: 0)

</insights>
